system_prompt:
You are a software system decomposition agent. Your task is to decompose a function block into smaller child function blocks.

CRITICAL RULES - ENFORCED:
1. EVERY CHILD MUST BE A FUNCTION, NOT A CLASS. Never generate class definitions for child blocks.
2. Each child must have explicit: name, purpose, inputs, outputs, and boundary
3. Preserve the parent's external interface - children's composition must match parent's inputs/outputs
4. TREE STRUCTURE (not graph): The decomposition forms a tree, not a graph. Children MUST NOT call each other (no cross-calls between siblings). The parent MUST explicitly and directly invoke all its children. A coordinator child node is ALLOWED, as long as it only coordinates work within its own subtree and never calls sibling nodes.
5. Do NOT add extra external inputs or outputs beyond what the parent has
6. Children should be at the same abstraction level and minimally overlapping

YOU MUST DECOMPOSE. You are NOT allowed to return 0 children or refuse to decompose. Even if the node seems simple, you MUST break it into at least 2 child functions.

OUTPUT FORMAT - You MUST return valid JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "children": [
    {
      "name": "ChildName",
      "purpose": "Clear description of what this child function does",
      "inputs": [{"name": "param", "type": "str", "description": "desc", "source": "where data comes from"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "int", "description": "desc", "consumer": "who uses this output"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["..."], "out_of_scope": ["..."]},
      "behavior": "Detailed description of expected behavior - how this function transforms inputs to outputs",
      "signature": "def ChildName(param1: type1, param2: type2) -> return_type",
      "stop_decompose": false,
      "stop_reason": "",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "data_store_name", "op": "read|write|read_write", "description": "what operation is needed"}
      ]
    }
  ],
  "decomposition_rationale": "Explain HOW these children work together to implement the parent."
}
user_prompt:
Decompose the following function block:

Node Name: Project Task Manager
Node Purpose: Project Task Manager

SubPRD Context:
  Task Description:
    Inputs: command: str, data: dict

    Functional Requirements:
    [FR-001] Create Project: Create a new project with name, description, and owner. Check that owner exists. Default status 'planning'. Record creation time.
    [FR-002] Update Project: Update project info: description, owner, status, deadline. Check project exists.
    [FR-003] Delete Project: Delete project and all its tasks. Check project exists.
    [FR-004] Create Task: Create a task under a project with title, description, priority, estimated hours. Check project exists and status is planning or active. Default status 'todo'. No assignee.
    [FR-005] Assign Task: Assign a task to a team member. Check task exists and unassigned or reassignable. Check member exists and available. Optionally check skills. Update assignee_id. Set member status to busy.
    [FR-006] Update Task Status: Update task status. Check task exists. Status flow: todo->in_progress->review->done. Blocked can be set anytime. Record actual hours when done.
    [FR-007] Complete Task: Complete a task. Check status is 'review'. Update status to 'done'. Record actual hours. Release member (set available).
    [FR-008] Delete Task: Delete a task. Check task exists. If assigned, release member.
    [FR-009] List Project Tasks: List tasks of a project with optional filters: status, priority, assignee. Show task details and progress.
    [FR-010] Get Member Tasks: Get all tasks for a member, current and historical. Calculate total hours.
    [FR-011] Get Project Progress: Get project progress: total tasks, completion rate, status counts, estimated vs actual hours, estimated completion time.
    [FR-012] Add Member: Add a team member with ID, name, role, skills, availability. Check member ID is unique.
    [FR-013] Update Member Availability: Update member availability. Check that member has no ongoing task before setting offline.

  Constraints:
    - NFR-001: Data must be consistent across the three in-memory data sources (members, projects, tasks) during operations like assignment, status updates, and deletions.
    - NFR-002: Error handling must cover invalid project/task/member, status conflicts, and state violations with appropriate error messages.

  Acceptance Criteria:
    - AC-001: Create project: owner must exist; project_id auto-generated; status defaults to 'planning'; creation time recorded.
    - AC-002: Update project: only existing projects can be updated; owner assignment checks existence; status changes are recorded.
    - AC-003: Delete project: project must exist; all associated tasks are deleted; if tasks were assigned, members are released.
    - AC-004: Create task: project must exist and be in 'planning' or 'active' status; task defaults to 'todo' and no assignee.
    - AC-005: Assign task: task must exist and be unassigned; member must exist and be 'available'; skill match is optional; assignee_id set; member status becomes 'busy'.
    - AC-006: Update task status: task must exist; status transitions follow the prescribed flow (todo->in_progress->review->done); blocked can be set anytime; actual hours recorded on done.
    - AC-007: Complete task: task must be in 'review' status; status changes to 'done'; actual hours recorded; assignee's availability set to 'available'.
    - AC-008: Delete task: task must exist; if assigned, member is released.
    - AC-009: List project tasks: returns tasks filtered by status, priority, assignee if provided; shows task details.
    - AC-010: Get member tasks: returns current and completed tasks for member; total hours computed correctly.
    - AC-011: Get project progress: returns total tasks, completion rate (completed/total), status counts, estimated vs actual hours, and estimated completion time.
    - AC-012: Add member: new member must have unique member_id; fields as specified.
    - AC-013: Update member availability: member must exist; cannot set offline if member has ongoing tasks.

    INPUT FORMAT:
      Format: json
      Description: Input is a JSON object with 'command' and 'project_data' fields. The 'project_data' contains parameters varying by command.
      Schema:
        command: string (enum: create_project, update_project, delete_project, create_task, assign_task, update_task_status, complete_task, delete_task, list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress, add_member, update_member_availability)
        project_data: {...}
          name: string (for create_project, create_task)
          description: string
          owner_id: integer (for create_project, update_project)
          project_id: integer (for project and task operations)
          task_id: integer (for task operations)
          assignee_id: integer (for assign_task)
          member_id: integer (for member operations)
          status: string (for update_project, update_task_status, update_member_availability)
          priority: string (for create_task)
          estimated_hours: integer (for create_task)
          actual_hours: integer (for complete_task)
          deadline: string (ISO date, for update_project)
          role: string (for add_member)
          skills: list of strings (for add_member)
          availability: string (for update_member_availability)
          status_filter: string (for list_project_tasks)
          priority_filter: string (for list_project_tasks)
          assignee_filter: integer (for list_project_tasks)
      Examples:
        {"command": "create_project", "project_data": {"name": "网站重构", "description": "重构公司官网", "owner_id": 6}}

    OUTPUT FORMAT:
      Format: json
      Description: Output is a JSON object with 'success', 'message', and 'data' fields.
      Examples:
        {"success": true, "message": "项目创建成功", "data": {"project_id": 1, "status": "planning"}}

Inputs:
  - command: str - Command to execute
  - data: dict - Data for the command

Outputs:
  - result: dict - JSON with success, message, data

Global Variables (MUST be DISTRIBUTED to children):
  - read_write on members: Stores team member information. Pre-populated with 6 members.
  - read_write on projects: Stores project information. Initially empty.
  - read_write on tasks: Stores task records. Initially empty.

  >>> Each child must declare a SUBSET of these global_vars in the 'global_vars' field. <<<
  >>> Children perform actual data operations; parent orchestrates by calling children. <<<

Available Data Interfaces (children should declare which they need in 'requested_capabilities'):
  - members.get: def get_member(member_id: int) -> Optional[dict]:
    Description: Retrieve a member by member_id
  - members.list: def list_members(**filters) -> list[dict]:
    Description: List members with optional filters (e.g., role, availability)
  - members.create: def create_member(data: dict) -> dict:
    Description: Create a new member with auto-generated member_id
  - members.update: def update_member(member_id: int, data: dict) -> dict:
    Description: Update an existing member with given fields
  - members.delete: def delete_member(member_id: int) -> bool:
    Description: Delete a member by member_id
  - members.exists: def exists_member(member_id: int) -> bool:
    Description: Check if a member exists by member_id
  - projects.get: def get_project(project_id: int) -> Optional[dict]:
    Description: Retrieve a project by project_id
  - projects.list: def list_projects(**filters) -> list[dict]:
    Description: List projects with optional filters (e.g., status, owner_id)
  - projects.create: def create_project(data: dict) -> dict:
    Description: Create a new project with auto-generated project_id
  - projects.update: def update_project(project_id: int, data: dict) -> dict:
    Description: Update an existing project with given fields
  - projects.delete: def delete_project(project_id: int) -> bool:
    Description: Delete a project by project_id
  - projects.exists: def exists_project(project_id: int) -> bool:
    Description: Check if a project exists by project_id
  - tasks.get: def get_task(task_id: int) -> Optional[dict]:
    Description: Retrieve a task by task_id
  - tasks.list: def list_tasks(**filters) -> list[dict]:
    Description: List tasks with optional filters (e.g., project_id, status, priority, assignee_id)
  - tasks.create: def create_task(data: dict) -> dict:
    Description: Create a new task with auto-generated task_id
  - tasks.update: def update_task(task_id: int, data: dict) -> dict:
    Description: Update an existing task with given fields
  - tasks.delete: def delete_task(task_id: int) -> bool:
    Description: Delete a task by task_id
  - tasks.exists: def exists_task(task_id: int) -> bool:
    Description: Check if a task exists by task_id

  >>> Each child that needs data access MUST list the required interfaces in 'requested_capabilities'. <<<

Maximum children allowed: 10
Maximum depth: 3
Return ONLY the JSON response.

children.name: ProjectWrite
children.purpose: Handle create_project and update_project commands, ensuring data consistency and owner existence.

inputs.name: action
inputs.source: derived from command field in parent input

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Dictionary containing fields like name, description, owner_id, status, deadline

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Create project with validation of owner existence
Update project fields with validation of project and owner existence

children.behavior: If action is 'create', check owner exists via members.exists, then call projects.create with provided data plus default status 'planning' and current timestamp. If action is 'update', check project exists via projects.exists, check owner exists if provided, then call projects.update with provided fields. Return appropriate success/error response.
children.signature: def ProjectWrite(action: str, project_data: dict) -> dict

children.name: DeleteProject
children.purpose: Handle delete_project command, deleting the project and all its tasks, releasing assigned members.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: ID of the project to delete

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Delete project by ID
Delete all tasks under the project
Release members assigned to those tasks

children.behavior: Check project exists via projects.exists. If not, return error. Retrieve all tasks for project via tasks.list, for each task if assignee_id is set, update member status to 'available' via members.update. Then delete all tasks via tasks.delete. Finally delete project via projects.delete. Return success.

children.name: CreateTask
children.purpose: Handle create_task command, creating a task under a valid project.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data containing task_id and status

in_scope: Validate project exists and status is planning or active
Create task with default status 'todo' and no assignee

children.behavior: Check project exists via projects.exists and its status is 'planning' or 'active' via projects.get. If not, return error. Then call tasks.create with provided data plus default status 'todo' and empty assignee_id. Return success with task_id.
children.signature: def CreateTask(project_id: int, title: str, description: str, priority: str, estimated_hours: int) -> dict

children.name: AssignTask
children.purpose: Handle assign_task command, assigning a task to a member.

inputs.name: assignee_id
inputs.description: ID of the member to assign

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Validate task exists and is unassigned
Validate member exists and is available
Optional skill match
Update task assignee_id
Set member status to busy

children.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.exists and that assignee_id is None or empty via tasks.get. Check member exists via members.exists and availability is 'available' via members.get. If either fails, return error. Optionally check skills if required (not specified in input, but can be assumed from data.skills? Actually, input doesn't have skills, so skip). Then call tasks.update to set assignee_id. Call members.update to set availability to 'busy'. Return success.
children.signature: def AssignTask(task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> dict

children.name: UpdateTaskStatus
children.purpose: Handle update_task_status command, updating task status with state machine validation.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Validate task exists
Validate status transition according to 'todo -> in_progress -> review -> done', or 'blocked' allowed anytime
Record actual hours only when status becomes 'done' (actual_hours from data)

children.behavior: Get current task via tasks.get. Validate transition: if new_status is not 'blocked', check that (current_status, new_status) is in allowed transitions. If invalid, return error. Then call tasks.update with new_status. If new_status is 'done', also update actual_hours if provided in data (from parent).
children.signature: def UpdateTaskStatus(task_id: int, new_status: str, actual_hours: int = None) -> dict

children.name: CompleteTask
children.purpose: Handle complete_task command, completing a task and releasing the assigned member.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Validate task exists and status is 'review'
Update status to 'done'
Record actual hours
Release member (set availability to 'available')

children.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.exists and current status is 'review'. If not, return error. Call tasks.update to set status 'done' and actual_hours. If task has assignee_id, call members.update to set availability to 'available'. Return success.
children.signature: def CompleteTask(task_id: int, actual_hours: int) -> dict

children.name: DeleteTask
children.purpose: Handle delete_task command, deleting a task and releasing its assignee if any.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Validate task exists
If assigned, release member
Delete task

children.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.exists. If not, return error. Get task details via tasks.get. If assignee_id is set, call members.update to set availability to 'available'. Then call tasks.delete. Return success.

children.name: ListTasks
children.purpose: Handle list_project_tasks and get_member_tasks commands, returning filtered task lists.

inputs.name: filters
inputs.description: Dictionary with optional keys: project_id, member_id, status, priority, assignee_id
inputs.source: derived from data fields in parent input

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data containing list of tasks and optionally total hours

in_scope: List tasks by project with optional filters
List tasks by member with total hours calculation

children.behavior: If filters contain project_id, call tasks.list with project_id and other filters. If filters contain member_id, call tasks.list with assignee_id = member_id. For member tasks, also calculate total actual_hours from returned tasks. Return list and optionally total_hours.

children.name: GetProjectProgress
children.purpose: Handle get_project_progress command, computing project statistics.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data containing progress metrics

in_scope: Count total tasks, completed tasks, status counts
Sum estimated vs actual hours
Estimate completion time based on rate

children.behavior: Check project exists. Get all tasks for project via tasks.list. Compute total tasks, completed (status='done') count, status counts, sum estimated_hours, sum actual_hours (if completed). Estimate completion time: if all done, actual time; otherwise, compute rate from completed tasks and apply to remaining. Return data.

children.name: MemberWrite

inputs.name: action
inputs.source: derived from command field in parent input

inputs.name: member_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with fields like member_id, name, role, skills, availability

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success, message, and data

in_scope: Add member with unique member_id
Update member availability, check no ongoing tasks before setting offline

children.behavior: If action is 'add', check if member_id is unique via members.exists, then call members.create. If action is 'update_availability', get member via members.get, check if new availability is 'offline' and member has any ongoing task (tasks.list with assignee_id and status not 'done'), if so error, else call members.update. Return success.
children.signature: def MemberWrite(action: str, member_data: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check ongoing tasks for availability update

response.decomposition_rationale: The Project Task Manager is decomposed into 10 child functions covering all 13 commands. Each child handles a specific set of related operations, ensuring separation of concerns and data consistency. The parent orchestrates by parsing the command and calling the appropriate child with extracted parameters. Children operate independently on global data stores via provided interfaces, without cross-calling each other.
